Okay, welcome to the final confident appeal, otherwise known as closing the question loop. So the final confident appeal helps close the question loop be opened up at the very beginning and gives your prospect a chance to borrow courage and confidence from you as you stand by your product and their need for it with absolute conviction. Now I want you to imagine if you've ever had a friend who convinced you to do something scary just by means of their own confidence and assurance. That's what you got to give your prospect. It should be a place where you experience reverse FOMO. So the reverse fear of missing out. It's that energy that you want to have here. You should legitimately feel a little scared of them missing out. These people, these prospects reading will continue to suffer and struggle unless they join you. So if you do care about the impact you or your client is creating, this is something you should take personally, at least a little. So let them feel not only your confidence, but your fear in them missing out when you could have helped. So remember, if you are connected to the moment of highest tension, you will want to solve it so badly as if it were your own. And that's the energy we want to write with when it comes to this final close. So let's take a look at our basic template for closing the question loop. So you are going to start by closing the question loop you raise in your top headline. You're going to confirm your product as the mechanism that answers the question. And then you're going to do what I call a conviction blast. So with total confidence, you are going to list out the type of people and what they're experiencing who need your product. So the best way to show this is to just read you an example in real time. So back to our friends at Soul Powered. By now, you already know that the biggest difference, so we used the difference headline at the beginning of the sales sales page. So by now, you already know that the biggest difference between predictable stress free growth and flatlining profits with a side order of intense burnout all comes down to your indestructible team. So if you're still here, let's wrap up with a real talk. So it all comes down to your indestructible team. That's the mechanism and product that answers that initial question. If you're an entrepreneur, executive or founder whose primary responsibility is leading the growth of a fast scaling company, you need the indestructible team. So that's the first part of our conviction blast. Now we just continue and pile it on. If you plan to bring even one new team member on this quarter, you need the indestructible team. If you're thinking of letting go of even one team member this quarter, you need the indestructible team. So already in these first three, essentially everyone should have something to grab onto. And we continue. If you've been wearing too many hats, feel stretched too thin and are tired of working in your business, you need the indestructible team. If you've woken up at least one morning this week in panic mode, having to put out the fire for someone who didn't do their job, you need the indestructible team. And if you've got your site set on sustainable long term growth and the pursuit of new opportunities, you need the indestructible team. So if you're ready, we look forward to partnering with you to make this the most rewarding and impactful 12 weeks. Your business has experienced all year. Another supplement you might want to look at here would be the Piglet, which is inside 10X launches as a bonus session in the office hours recording. Now this is all about reverse FOMO and just pouring on that conviction blast with the same desperation you would feel as if you were experiencing that pain and needed it extinguished immediately. I want you to give that a look, give that supplement a look if you feel called for a little more information, but whenever you already take a few moments to write your final competent appeal and really blast that conviction in the final lines. Even if you're a little embarrassed and shaky about doing that in the past, this is where you really have to go all in and stand by your product in a very strong way. So give that a go and I will see you in the next lesson for a bonus close called the risk mitigator. I'll see you there.
